audit_result.proof_trace: blstrs::G1Point::from_compressed accepts the identity point (point at infinity) as a valid compressed G1 element, returning Some(G1Point::identity()) rather than None. The code at L77-80 propagates this without explicit identity validation, allowing identity commitments to pass verification.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Attacker sends blob with commitment=0xc000...0000 (identity point). deserialize_compressed_g1 succeeds, verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch proceeds with identity commitment. Pairing checks may accept trivial proofs since e(O,g2)=1 for identity O, bypassing cryptographic guarantees.

PROP-57888860-inv-051.text: KZG commitment validation must reject the point at infinity (identity element of the elliptic curve group) as an invalid commitment. A commitment equal to the identity point does not correspond to any valid polynomial and must not pass verification.
PROP-57888860-inv-051.assertion: forall commitment c in verify_cell_kzg_proof or verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch: c != G1_IDENTITY (point at infinity) => reject
